target.title: feat(gateway): add LLM request context headers for AI gateway integra…
target.body:
…tion

Propagate session, attempt, and turn-level context via HTTP headers to enable AI gateways to extract tracing/billing information without JWT parsing.

Headers injected:
- X-OpenClaw-Session-Id: session identifier
- X-OpenClaw-Attempt-Id: attempt (run) identifier
- X-OpenClaw-Fallback: "true" when using fallback model after primary failure
- X-OpenClaw-Turn-Index: monotonically increasing turn index per LLM call
- X-OpenClaw-Turn-Id: unique turn identifier ({attemptId}-turn-{index})

The turn-level headers are injected via streamFn wrapper to ensure each HTTP request gets a unique index, since one prompt() call may trigger multiple LLM HTTP requests (one per tool-result round).

Also adds `isFallback` field to PluginHookLlmOutputEvent for plugin visibility into fallback model usage.

target.title: Control UI: add toggle to expand tool call output by default in webchat (fixes #54478)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** In the webchat (Control UI), tool call output is always collapsed by default. Users must manually click to expand each output, which is inconvenient when debugging or tracking agent workflows.
- **Why it matters:** The TUI already supports this via `Ctrl+O` / `/verbose on`, but the webchat has no equivalent toggle.
- **What changed:** Added a new `chatExpandToolCalls` setting with a toggle button in the chat controls toolbar (both desktop and mobile). When enabled, all `<details>` elements wrapping tool calls and tool outputs render with the `open` attribute, expanding them by default.
- **What did NOT change:** Backend, session data, tool call rendering logic, or existing `showToolCalls` (show/hide) behavior. The expand toggle is disabled when tool calls are hidden.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54478

## Root Cause / Regression History

N/A — new feature.

## Regression Test Plan

N/A — new feature.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New toggle button (unfold icon) in chat controls toolbar, next to the existing tool calls toggle.
- When active, all tool call sections and tool output sections are expanded by default.
- Setting is persisted in localStorage.
- Toggle is disabled when tool calls are hidden (`chatShowToolCalls: false`).

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

All 35 chat tests + 1 gateway session test pass. Settings persistence tested via existing storage test patterns.

## Human Verification

- Verified: `chatExpandToolCalls` defaults to `false`, toggles correctly, persists across reloads.
- Edge cases: disabled state when `showToolCalls=false`, onboarding mode behavior.
- Not verified: live gateway with real tool call messages (no access to running instance).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes (new setting defaults to `false`, matching current behavior)
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery

- How to disable/revert: Revert this commit; tool calls will return to always-collapsed default.
- Known bad symptoms: If `chatExpandToolCalls` is missing from persisted settings, it gracefully defaults to `false`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Large conversations with many tool calls may render slower when all expanded.
  - Mitigation: Default is `false` (collapsed); user opts in explicitly.

## Files Changed (11)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `ui/src/ui/storage.ts` | Added `chatExpandToolCalls` to `UiSettings`, defaults, parsing, persistence |
| `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts` | Added `expandToolCalls` opt, `?open=` on `<details>` elements |
| `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` | Added `expandToolCalls` to `ChatProps`, passed to `renderMessageGroup` |
| `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` | Derived `expandToolCalls` from settings, passed to chat view |
| `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts` | Added expand toggle button (desktop + mobile) with unfold icon |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts` | Added `expandToolCallsToggle` i18n string |
| 5 test files | Added `chatExpandToolCalls: false` / `expandToolCalls: false` to test fixtures |

target.title: Config: plumb opt-in SSRF policy for web fetch, citation redirects, and remote media
target.body:
## Summary
This PR converts the previously proven dist-level SSRF benchmark-range hotfix into a source-level, config-driven, opt-in implementation.

It adds shared `ssrfPolicy` config plumbing for three scoped paths:

- `tools.web.fetch.ssrfPolicy`
- `tools.web.search.citationRedirect.ssrfPolicy`
- `messages.remoteMedia.ssrfPolicy`

It also fixes legacy web search normalization so `tools.web.search.citationRedirect` is preserved instead of being dropped during normalization.

## Why
Some public endpoints can resolve into RFC 2544 benchmarking / special-use address space and were being blocked as false positives by SSRF handling.

The goal here is **not** to broaden defaults or add domain-specific exceptions, but to expose existing shared SSRF policy controls in the right runtime/config surfaces so operators can opt in per subsystem.

## What changed
- Added shared SSRF policy schema/type reuse for:
  - `tools.web.fetch.ssrfPolicy`
  - `tools.web.search.citationRedirect.ssrfPolicy`
  - `messages.remoteMedia.ssrfPolicy`
- Threaded fetch SSRF policy into the `web_fetch` guarded fetch path
- Threaded citation redirect SSRF policy into Gemini citation redirect resolution
- Threaded remote media SSRF policy into outbound remote media loading paths
- Preserved `citationRedirect` during legacy web-search normalization
- Added focused tests for:
  - guarded fetch SSRF policy forwarding
  - citation redirect SSRF policy forwarding
  - legacy normalization retention
  - outbound media option passthrough

## Non-goals
- No broad hardcoded allowlists
- No domain-specific exceptions
- No Discord reconnect/lifecycle changes
- No unrelated refactors

## Validation
Scoped validation performed:
- `src/agents/tools/web-guarded-fetch.test.ts`
- `src/agents/tools/web-search.redirect.test.ts`
- `src/config/legacy-web-search.test.ts`
- `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
- `src/plugin-sdk/outbound-media.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:schema:check`

target.title: CLI: add stable setup surface export for dynamic provider/channel configuration UIs
target.body:
# CLI: add a stable setup surface export for dynamic provider/channel configuration UIs

## Summary

This PR adds a stable, machine-readable setup surface export for OpenClaw so external UIs can render provider and channel configuration dynamically without importing internal runtime chunks or scraping interactive CLI output.

Today, setup metadata already exists across:

- plugin manifests
- provider auth choices
- channel setup wizards
- setup-only plugin entries

But OpenClaw does not yet expose a supported serialized contract for exporting that setup information.
As a result, downstream UIs either hardcode a small subset of flows or depend on brittle internal implementation details.

This PR adds a minimal, stable, read-only export seam without changing the behavior of existing `setup` or `configure` commands.

## Problem

An external configuration UI that wants to track OpenClaw's real supported surface needs to know:

- which providers and channels are available
- how they should be labeled and ordered
- whether they are installed or configured
- which fields they require
- whether setup is token-based, env-based, browser/oauth-based, polling-based, or multi-step
- which status, hints, and docs should be displayed

Current interfaces are not enough:

- `openclaw plugins list --json` is plugin inventory, not setup surface
- `openclaw configure` is interactive, not machine-readable
- importing internal `dist` chunks is unstable across builds and versions
- current setup wizards are executable JS objects, not a public JSON contract

That leaves downstream UIs stuck with two weak choices:

- hardcode provider/channel flows
- depend on internal implementation details that may break on upgrade

## Proposal

Add a dedicated setup surface export command.

Suggested form:

- `openclaw configure surface --json-out <file>`

Optional flags may include:

- `--section providers|channels`
- `--installed-only`
- `--workspace <dir>`

This command writes a complete setup surface document to a JSON file for external UIs to consume.

A key design principle is:

- machine consumers must not depend on clean stdout
- the supported protocol should be the output file

That avoids JSON corruption from mixed logs or warnings.

## Why this is low risk

This PR:

- does not change existing `openclaw setup`
- does not change existing `openclaw configure`
- does not change existing plugin setup behavior
- only adds a read-only export seam
- allows external UIs to stop depending on internal chunk exports

This is not a rewrite of configure/onboarding. It is a formal export for setup surfaces that already exist.

## Why this fits the plugin/extensibility model

This is not an app-specific special case. It formalizes OpenClaw's existing plugin-defined setup capabilities.

In other words:

- channel plugins continue to define setup via `ChannelSetupWizard` and setup entries
- provider plugins continue to define auth/setup via provider auth choices and provider metadata
- core only serializes those existing setup surfaces into stable descriptors
- any UI can consume the same export, not just one client

This strengthens the plugin architecture and makes plugin-defined setup surfaces reusable.

## Suggested exported content

For each provider or channel, export:

- `id`
- `kind`
  - `provider` or `channel`
- `label`
- `detailLabel`
- `selectionLabel`
- `blurb`
- `docsPath`
- `systemImage` when available
- `installed`
- `configured`
- `selectionHint`
- `statusLines`
- `quickstartScore`
- `fields`
  - serializable field descriptors derived from credentials, text inputs, env shortcuts, etc.
- `actions`
  - serializable action descriptors such as submit, open browser, poll, install plugin, etc.
- `capabilities`
  - for example multi-account, oauth, polling, env shortcut, gateway-required

The focus of this PR is exporting setup surface descriptors, not rewriting setup execution semantics.

## Non-goals

This PR does not attempt to:

- replace existing interactive `configure`
- redesign setup wizard internals
- expose setup wizard JS objects directly as public API
- expose unstable internal runtime module layouts
- add app-specific behavior for a single client

## Implementation approach

Minimal implementation path:

1. Define serializable setup surface descriptor types.
2. Implement an exporter that builds descriptors from existing provider/channel setup metadata.
3. Add a `configure surface` command.
4. Support `--json-out`.
5. Keep logs on stderr and let machine consumers rely only on the output file.

## Why `--json-out` instead of stdout-only JSON

In some environments, stdout may include logs, warnings, or diagnostics.
If the protocol depends on perfectly clean stdout, integrations become brittle.

File output is a better long-term machine interface because it avoids:

- stdout contamination
- JSON extraction/cleanup heuristics
- inconsistent behavior across environments

For that reason, this PR proposes:

- `--json-out <file>`

as the supported machine interface.

## Value for downstream UIs

With this export, external UIs can:

- render all providers dynamically
- render all channels dynamically
- stop maintaining hardcoded setup lists
- track OpenClaw's supported surface across upgrades

This is especially useful for onboarding flows and settings dashboards.

## Compatibility

- existing CLI usage remains unchanged
- existing plugins do not need immediate changes
- older clients can ignore the new command completely
- newer clients can depend on the formal seam instead of internal implementation details

## Why this is better than client-side workarounds

Without this seam, downstream clients are forced to choose between:

- hardcoded setup UIs
- importing internal chunks
- parsing partial plugin inventory
- scraping interactive CLI output

All of these are weaker contracts than adding a small, explicit export interface.

## Possible follow-ups

This PR is intentionally the smallest useful step. Future work can build on it with:

- richer provider setup surface export
- setup session execution APIs
- gateway/RPC access to setup surfaces
- more formal plugin-sdk public types

But this PR itself stays intentionally narrow:

- a stable, read-only, machine-consumable setup surface export

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.channels.ts src/config/types.discord.ts src/config/types.notifications.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.channels-config.ts src/config/zod-schema.notifications.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.ts src/plugin-sdk/system-event-runtime.ts`
  - `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Evidence after fix:
  Redacted terminal output from the live Discord API canary for this PR's reaction listener path:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

  - Focused regression tests passed: 3 files, 92 tests.
  - Generated channel config check passed.
  - Generated docs baseline check passed with `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
  - Formatter reported all 21 touched files use the correct format.
  - Git whitespace checks exited cleanly.
  - Typecheck passed for core and extensions through `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
  - Plugin contracts passed: 62 files, 857 tests.
- Observed result after fix: default Discord reaction handling queues exactly one system event and does not request heartbeat; opt-in `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests `source: "notifications-event"`, `intent: "immediate"`, `reason: "notification-wake:discord-reaction"`, the routed `agentId`, and routed `sessionKey`; `off` skips enqueue; duplicate enqueue does not wake; custom emoji retain stable `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` context keys; `notification-wake:*` preflight bypasses empty `HEARTBEAT.md` skip while generic notification events do not.
- What was not tested: no production Discord Gateway restart was performed after this follow-up refactor. The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous patch woke every accepted Discord reaction immediately, which made the behavior too aggressive and inconsistent with the shared notification policy in #83611.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction notification enqueue was wired directly to heartbeat wake behavior instead of going through a configurable notification wake policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered reaction enqueue/routing but did not distinguish queue-by-default, opt-in wake, disabled enqueue, deduped no-wake, or empty-`HEARTBEAT.md` notification wake preflight.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord and Telegram reaction PRs were converging on similar semantics without a shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests or files:
  - `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: shared policy resolution precedence, queue default, explicit wake, explicit off, deduped no-wake, Discord listener wiring, custom emoji stable keys, and notification wake preflight through empty `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests exercise the shared helper and Discord listener seam directly without broad channel startup or live gateway flake.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. System event text keeps stable `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` and `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` markers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued] -> [notification-wake heartbeat]

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor harness and shared notification/heartbeat runtime tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default reaction policy, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and `notificationWake.reactions: "off"`

### Steps

1. Run the focused notification, Discord monitor, and heartbeat preflight tests.
2. Run generated config/docs checks.
3. Run formatter, whitespace, typecheck, and plugin contract checks.

### Expected

- Accepted reactions queue by default.
- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

- Passed as described in the proof section.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.
- What you did **not** verify: production Discord Gateway delivery after this follow-up refactor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Optional new `notificationWake.reactions` config
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators expecting every reaction to wake immediately need an explicit opt-in.
  - Mitigation: default queue preserves visibility without surprise model wakes; `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` restores immediate processing intentionally.
- Risk: notification wake preflight could over-bypass empty `HEARTBEAT.md` gating.
  - Mitigation: the bypass is limited to `source: "notifications-event"` plus `reason` prefix `notification-wake:`; generic notification events still skip.

target.title: fix: align codex simple completions with responses API
target.body:
This PR was created with AI assistance.

## Summary
- pass simple-completion instructions via `systemPrompt` for auto topic labels and direct TTS summarization
- omit `temperature` only for `openai-codex-responses` while keeping existing behavior for other simple-completion backends
- surface simple-completion errors for auto topic label generation and add regression coverage

## Why
`openai-codex-responses` expects instructions in the request's `instructions` field, which the simple completion transport derives from `systemPrompt`.

These call sites were previously embedding the instruction text into the user message instead. That works for some simple-completion backends, but it causes Codex Responses requests to fail because no `instructions` value is sent. In practice, auto topic label generation would then fall back to an empty label, and direct summarization could also fail on the same path.

This change keeps the fix local to OpenClaw's simple-completion call sites instead of changing shared provider behavior.

## Testing
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/tts/tts.test.ts -t "passes summary instructions via systemPrompt and omits temperature"`

target.title: ACP: add gateway-owned node-backed runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw did not yet have a complete **node-backed ACP runtime** where the gateway owns the durable state and a paired node acts only as the execution worker.
- Why it matters: without that split, restart/replay/reconnect behavior is too dependent on the live process, which makes lease fencing, terminal resolution, and outward delivery recovery harder to reason about and prove.
- What this PR adds:
  - a durable ACP gateway store for sessions, runs, accepted worker events, leases, terminal state, replay targets, and projector checkpoints
  - gateway ingestion of ACP worker events coming back from paired nodes
  - a real node-host ACP worker bridge and gateway/node command plumbing
  - a durable ACP reply projection/replay path so restart recovery can resume from gateway state instead of only from the live iterator
  - startup recovery for incomplete ACP node runs
  - restart/reconnect/cancel/close race hardening with focused restart/replay test coverage
  - architecture / protocol / verification docs for the new backend
- Landing-only follow-up: after rebasing onto current `main`, this PR also includes a few **test-only** isolation fixes in Feishu / Google / Google Chat extension tests that were needed to make the landing branch green. Those are not ACP runtime behavior changes.

## What changed by area

### 1) Durable ACP gateway state

New ACP store/state plumbing under `src/acp/store/**`:

- persists ACP sessions and runs
- persists accepted worker events
- tracks leases / lease epochs
- resolves canonical terminal state on the gateway
- stores replay targets and projector checkpoints separately from the runtime cursor

This is the foundation that lets the gateway stay authoritative even when execution happens on a paired node.

### 2) Gateway ↔ node ACP worker path

Main implementation areas:

- `src/gateway/**`
- `src/infra/node-commands.ts`
- `src/node-host/**`

This adds the node-backed ACP execution path:

- gateway can start/control ACP work on a paired node
- node-host can run the ACP worker role
- worker events stream back to the gateway over the existing node transport
- gateway remains the source of truth for run state and terminal resolution

### 3) Durable reply projection and replay

Main implementation areas:

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-replay.ts`

This PR moves ACP outward delivery onto a durable replayable path:

- reply targets are persisted per run
- projector checkpoints are advanced only after successful delivery
- restart recovery can replay the unfinished suffix from durable state
- reconnect and duplicate accepted events do not re-deliver already completed outward effects

### 4) Startup recovery / reconnect handling

Main implementation areas:

- `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`
- `src/acp/runtime/acp-node.ts`

This adds the gateway-side recovery flow needed to:

- resume incomplete projection after restart
- keep reconnect handling deterministic
- prevent stale-worker wins
- preserve canonical terminal behavior across restart/replay windows

### 5) Test harnesses and regression coverage

Main test additions/expansions:

- `src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts`
- `src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts`
- `src/acp/test-harness/restart-harness.ts`

These cover the core failure windows this backend needs to survive:

- restart after accepted events but before replay checkpoint advancement
- restart after terminal persistence but before final outward convergence
- reconnect replay of already accepted events
- cancel / complete / close race handling
- restart recovery with a real memory cut instead of reusing in-process objects

### 6) Docs included in this PR

Main docs:

- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-protocol.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-verification.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-runtime-program.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-summary.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-final-judge.md`

These are included because this was developed as a staged architecture / verification effort and the final accepted behavior is documented there.

## Explicit scope boundaries / v1 limitations

This PR does **not**:

- turn nodes into mini-gateways
- add automatic cross-node continuation for an in-flight run
- claim durable resumed tool-message edit support after restart

Current conservative post-restart tool-update behavior remains:

- `append_only_after_restart`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: ACP node runtime architecture / protocol / verification docs under `docs/experiments/plans/`
- Related: landing-only extension test isolation fixes after rebase onto current `main`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- OpenClaw now has a first-class ACP node-backed runtime path where the gateway owns durable ACP truth and paired nodes execute the worker role.
- Gateway restart/recovery can resume incomplete ACP outward projection from durable state.
- Duplicate accepted worker-event replay on reconnect is hardened so it does not duplicate outward delivery.
- Post-restart tool updates intentionally follow the documented conservative v1 rule: `append_only_after_restart`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? Yes

### Security / trust-boundary notes

- Paired nodes can now advertise ACP worker capability and receive ACP runtime commands over the existing gateway↔node trust boundary.
- Gateway↔node websocket traffic now carries ACP worker control / event flows in addition to existing node commands.
- Gateway now persists ACP runtime state needed for durable replay/recovery.
- The design mitigates stale-worker / duplicate-delivery risks via lease epochs, canonical terminal resolution, durable replay checkpoints, and reconnect/replay handling on the gateway.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (landing worktree on vm1) + GitHub Actions CI
- Runtime/container: Node / pnpm; Bun build path also checked
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP gateway↔node runtime path
- Relevant config (redacted): ACP runtime restart/replay harnesses and paired-node fixtures

### Verified locally

- ACP targeted verification gate:
  - `pnpm test -- src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts src/acp/store/store.test.ts src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts src/acp/store/gateway-events.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.acp.test.ts`
- TypeScript guardrail:
  - `pnpm tsgo`
- Landing-only extension test fixes:
  - `pnpm test:extensions`

### CI / build notes

- `pnpm build` still fails only with the pre-existing Bun wrapper issue:
  - `Cannot find module './cjs/index.cjs' from ''`
- That build failure was already present and is not introduced by the ACP runtime changes in this PR.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the ACP-focused local gate listed above.
- Verified `pnpm tsgo` passes.
- Verified the rebased landing branch’s extension test-isolation fixes with local `pnpm test:extensions`.
- Verified the PR now includes the architecture / protocol / verification / final-judge docs for this backend.

What I did **not** verify:

- I did **not** get a clean `pnpm build` because of the pre-existing Bun wrapper failure noted above.
- I did **not** run a fresh manual end-to-end Discord/real-node interactive repro outside the checked-in restart/replay and node-host harnesses.

## Review Guide

If you want the shortest path through this PR, review in this order:

1. **Core architecture / intent**
   - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md`
   - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-protocol.md`
2. **Gateway-owned durable state**
   - `src/acp/store/**`
3. **Node execution bridge**
   - `src/node-host/**`
   - `src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.ts`
   - `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`
4. **Durable outward replay path**
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts`
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts`
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-replay.ts`
5. **Recovery and proofs**
   - `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.ts`
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts`
   - `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts`
6. **Ignore as landing-only test fixes unless you are checking CI fallout**
   - `extensions/feishu/**`
   - `extensions/google/**`
   - `extensions/googlechat/**`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No explicit migration required
- Migration needed? No

Notes:

- This adds a new ACP runtime/backend path; it does not force existing deployments to switch to it immediately.
- Post-restart tool updates intentionally remain append-only in v1.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR or avoid selecting/using the `acp-node` runtime path until fixed.
- Files/config to restore: ACP runtime/store/gateway/node-host changes under `src/acp/**`, `src/node-host/**`, `src/gateway/**`, plus the accompanying docs if reverting wholesale.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicate final delivery after restart, stale worker winning after reconnect, projector checkpoint drift, or replay targeting the wrong route.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: restart/replay correctness is subtle in any durable projection system.
  - Mitigation: this PR includes restart harnesses, startup recovery tests, reconnect/replay ordering tests, and explicit protocol/verification docs.
- Risk: the PR is large and spans multiple ACP runtime layers.
  - Mitigation: the review guide above splits it into durable store, node bridge, replay path, recovery path, and landing-only CI fixes.
- Risk: paired-node ACP execution expands the gateway↔node command surface.
  - Mitigation: the gateway remains authoritative for leases, terminal resolution, checkpoints, and replay instead of trusting node-local completion.

---

## AI Checklist

- [x] **AI-assisted** — agent-harness / Codex driven implementation, review, and landing follow-ups
- [x] **Testing degree** — focused local verification plus CI
- [x] **Understanding confirmed** — this PR adds the gateway-owned ACP node-backed runtime and includes a few landing-only extension test fixes after rebase
- [ ] Prompts/session logs — available on request

target.title: feat(routing): support per-binding workspace override
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an optional `workspace` field to route bindings so that the same agent can operate in different directories depending on which chat/peer it responds to.
- Threads the workspace through `ResolvedAgentRoute` so downstream consumers can use it.
- In `getReplyFromConfig`, binding workspace takes precedence over the agent-level default workspace.

### Motivation

In git-worktree workflows, each worktree lives in a separate directory but logically belongs to the same agent. Currently, the only way to point an agent at a different directory is to create a separate agent entry per workspace. With per-binding workspace, a single agent definition can serve multiple worktrees — each bound to a different chat/group via the existing binding mechanism.

**Example config:**
```json
{
  "bindings": [{
    "agentId": "coder",
    "workspace": "/path/to/feature-worktree-A",
    "match": { "channel": "slack", "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "C_CHANNEL_A" } }
  }, {
    "agentId": "coder",
    "workspace": "/path/to/feature-worktree-B",
    "match": { "channel": "slack", "peer": { "kind": "group", "id": "C_CHANNEL_B" } }
  }]
}
```

### Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.agents.ts` | Add `workspace?: string` to `AgentRouteBinding` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.agents.ts` | Add `workspace` to `RouteBindingSchema` |
| `src/routing/resolve-route.ts` | Thread `workspace` through `ResolvedAgentRoute` and `choose()` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts` | Add `resolveBindingWorkspace()` — binding workspace takes precedence |

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify existing routing tests pass (`pnpm test -- --grep routing`)
- [ ] Add a binding with `workspace` field and confirm the agent runs in that directory
- [ ] Confirm bindings without `workspace` behave identically to before (no regression)
- [ ] Verify `resolveBindingWorkspace` returns `undefined` when no binding matches


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix(discord): skip channels claimed by other instances
target.body:
## Summary

Add a lightweight shared Discord channel-claim registry so multi-instance deployments sharing the same bot token can silently skip channels owned by another instance instead of replying `This channel is not allowed.`.

## What changed

- add `src/discord/monitor/instance-claims.ts`
- register Discord claims on provider startup and refresh them periodically
- gate Discord slash command dispatch with per-instance channel ownership
- gate Discord message preflight with per-instance channel ownership
- add a focused test for instance-key derivation and claim ownership

## Why

Without shared ownership, a rescue/secondary instance can receive a slash interaction for a channel owned by the primary instance and reject it using its local allowlist. That causes:
- user-facing `This channel is not allowed.` on valid channels
- `already acknowledged` / `unknown interaction` on the intended owner instance

This PR makes the non-owner instance silently return instead.

## Scope

This PR intentionally does **not** change Discord interaction defer/reply timing. It only addresses cross-instance channel ownership.

## Testing

- added `src/discord/monitor.instance-claims.test.ts`
- local repo in this environment does not currently have dependencies installed, so full vitest/tsc was not runnable here
- live validation was done against two local OpenClaw instances (`~/.openclaw` + `~/.openclaw-rescue`) sharing one bot token; the incorrect deny path stopped reproducing after the claim gating patch

Closes #55651

target.title: Fix stale session routes for removed providers
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Prevents stale persisted session model/provider state from routing through a provider that is no longer visible to the current gateway model catalog/config.
- Applies the guard in `resolveSessionModelRef`, and wires `chat.send` to pass catalog visibility when provider rows, replace mode, or a non-default persisted session route can make stale providers risky.
- Covers explicit `providerOverride`/`modelOverride` and stale runtime `modelProvider`/`model` fields.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90471 reports sessions continuing to use a deleted provider after config changes, creating silent billing/quota risk.

What is the intended outcome?

- Persisted session routes are preserved when they match the configured default, an explicit configured provider row, or a loaded catalog model.
- Persisted session routes fall back to the current configured default when catalog-aware gateway routing cannot find the stored provider/model anymore, including when the deleted provider was the last custom provider row.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Adding a new user-facing session reset command.
- Changing provider catalog discovery or model picker behavior.
- Migrating or deleting `sessions.json` entries on disk.

What does success look like?

- Runtime chat routing no longer honors deleted-provider session state once the current catalog/config says the provider is gone.
- Valid merge-mode provider/plugin catalog routes outside `models.providers` continue to work.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the catalog-aware resolver guard now matches OpenClaw merge/replace model visibility semantics.
- Whether `chat.send` loads catalog visibility at the right point for persisted session route validation.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90471

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90462

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Not maintainer-requested; this addresses the open bug report and ClawSweeper's source-repro note on #90471.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Deleted provider session override/runtime state no longer wins over the current configured default route when the current model catalog cannot see the stored provider/model.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node v24.15.0, with the gateway session resolver imported through `tsx` after this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
};
const staleSession = {

  updatedAt: Date.now(),

  modelOverride: 'deepseek-v4-pro:cloud',
  modelProvider: 'ollama-cloud',
  model: 'deepseek-v4-pro:cloud',
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(resolveSessionModelRef(cfg, staleSession, undefined, { modelCatalog: [] }), null, 2));
EOF
```

```json
  "model": "deepseek-v4-pro"
}
```

- Additional last-custom-provider deletion proof run after the ClawSweeper review finding:

- Additional /stop command regression proof run after the latest ClawSweeper review finding:

- Observed result after fix: stale persisted override/runtime fields are ignored when absent from current catalog/config, including when there are no remaining custom provider rows, while a valid catalog-backed provider outside `models.providers` remains selected in default merge mode. Stop commands and fresh/default custom-provider sends now return before session model catalog validation is loaded.
- What was not tested: a live Windows gateway sending real paid-provider traffic; no paid provider credentials were used.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof exercises the actual gateway session resolver and catalog-load predicate used by chat send, but does not perform an external model API call.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the added regression tests encode the prior failure shape; before this patch, `resolveSessionModelRef` returned persisted override/runtime state before checking current provider visibility.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```sh

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`: passed, 2 files / 70 tests on the final run.

- `node scripts/run-additional-boundary-checks.mjs --shard=2/4,3/4,4/4`: passed after updating the qa-lab raw-fetch allowlist line numbers to match current main.
- Direct stale-provider proof command: returned `custom-api-deepseek-com/deepseek-v4-pro`.
- Direct last-custom-provider proof command: returned `shouldLoadCatalog: true` and `openai/gpt-5.4`.
- Direct merge-mode compatibility proof command: returned `plugin-provider/plugin-model`.
- Fresh/default custom-provider no-catalog proof: `chat.send` started successfully without calling `loadGatewayModelCatalog`; helper tests returned `false` for fresh, default persisted, and explicitly configured provider-row sessions.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added resolver tests for ignoring removed-provider explicit overrides.
- Added resolver tests for ignoring removed-provider runtime model fields.
- Added regressions for deleting the last custom provider row and falling back to a built-in/default provider.
- Added a preservation test for overrides whose provider still exists in explicit config.
- Added merge-mode preservation tests for built-in and catalog-backed providers outside `models.providers`.
- Added chat server coverage through the existing `chat.send` test shard after wiring catalog-aware session route resolution.
- Added a `chat.send` `/stop` regression proving stop commands bypass session model catalog validation.
- Added helper and `chat.send` regressions proving fresh/default custom-provider sends skip unnecessary catalog validation.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Source-level behavior: persisted override/runtime session model state was returned before validating whether the provider/model was still visible in current config/catalog, so deleted provider state could remain the effective route.

If no test was added, why not?

- Regression tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No config shape, environment, or migration behavior changed. Runtime route resolution now treats catalog-missing persisted session providers as invalid when chat routing has catalog visibility.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, provider/auth routing behavior changes for stale persisted session state.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Existing sessions with persisted routes to providers that are no longer configured or present in the loaded catalog will now fall back to the configured default instead of continuing to use that stale provider.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The guard preserves the configured default provider.
- The guard preserves providers still present in explicit config.
- Default merge mode remains permissive when no catalog is available and no non-default persisted route needs validation.
- Catalog-backed provider/plugin routes outside `models.providers` are preserved when catalog visibility is available.
- Focused tests cover removed-provider override/runtime paths, last-custom-provider deletion, and merge-mode provider preservation.

target.title: fix(webchat): slash menu clipped by overflow and missing keyboard scroll
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The webchat slash command menu (`/`) is completely invisible — typing `/` changes the input border color and arrow-key selection works, but the dropdown never appears. This is because `.agent-chat__input` uses `overflow: hidden` (for textarea scroll containment), which clips the absolutely-positioned slash menu that renders above the input via `position: absolute; bottom: 100%`.
- Additionally, when the slash menu has more items than the visible scroll area, keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/ArrowDown) does not auto-scroll the active item into view — users must manually scroll with the mouse.
- What changed: Introduced `.agent-chat__input-wrap` as a positioning parent for the slash menu, so it renders outside the `overflow: hidden` container. Added `scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })` after each arrow-key index change.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No modifications to slash command logic, command execution, or any non-UI code.

![slash-menu-fix](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9623f14a-9a7d-43a8-82fe-f75d5be85ba2)
- Here above demonstrates the fix.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Slash command menu now appears above the input box when typing `/` in webchat.
- Arrow-key navigation in the slash menu auto-scrolls to keep the active item visible.
- No changes to existing keyboard shortcuts (Tab to fill, Enter to select, Escape to close).

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin 25.2.0)
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22+
- Integration/channel: Webchat (Control UI)

### Steps

1. Start gateway: `openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force`
2. Open `http://127.0.0.1:18789/webchat/`
3. Type `/` in the chat input

### Expected

- Slash command menu appears above the input box
- ArrowDown/ArrowUp navigates through commands with auto-scroll

### Actual (before fix)

- Input border changes color (`:focus-within` fires), arrow keys + Enter work (DOM exists), but the menu is completely invisible — clipped by `overflow: hidden`
- When menu is made visible via other means, keyboard navigation past visible items requires manual mouse scrolling

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording

Before fix — typing `/` shows no menu (only border color change):
The DOM is present and functional (keyboard selection works), but the popup is clipped to zero height by `overflow: hidden` on the parent.

After fix — slash menu visible and keyboard-scrollable:
Menu appears above input; ArrowDown past visible items auto-scrolls the list.

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios:
  - Built UI locally via `pnpm ui:build`, started gateway with `node openclaw.mjs gateway run`
  - Confirmed slash menu appears when typing `/`
  - Confirmed ArrowDown/ArrowUp auto-scrolls active item into view
  - Confirmed Tab fills command, Enter selects, Escape closes
  - Confirmed input box border-radius, textarea scroll, and backdrop-filter unaffected
  - Confirmed mobile layout selector updated (`.agent-chat__input-wrap` in `layout.mobile.css`)
- What I did **not** verify:
  - macOS/iOS native app webchat (only tested browser webchat)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the `.agent-chat__input-wrap` wrapper — move `margin`/`flex-shrink` back to `.agent-chat__input` and remove the wrapper `<div>` in `chat.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: If a CSS rule targets `.agent-chat__input` for margin/positioning, it may need updating to `.agent-chat__input-wrap`.

## Files changed

- `ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css` — Added `.agent-chat__input-wrap` class; moved `margin` and `flex-shrink` from `.agent-chat__input` to the wrapper
- `ui/src/styles/layout.mobile.css` — Updated mobile override selector from `.agent-chat__input` to `.agent-chat__input-wrap`
- `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` — Wrapped input bar in `.agent-chat__input-wrap`; moved `renderSlashMenu` outside `.agent-chat__input`; added `scrollSlashMenuActiveIntoView()` helper called after ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigation

**AI-assisted:** Built with Claude Code. Tested manually on local gateway webchat.

target.title: fix: block empty exec commands + consecutive tool-error circuit breaker (plus minor UI test stability)
target.body:
**Summary**
- Add a guard that blocks `exec`/`bash` tool calls when the `command` parameter is missing or blank, preventing empty-command execution loops.
- Add a consecutive tool-error counter and a circuit-breaker callback in the embedded subscribe tool handler chain; trip after 3 consecutive tool errors.
- Stabilize UI `formatNextRun()` weekday output across locales so tests don’t fail on non-English environments.

**Motivation / Context**
- Following `exec-loop-fix.md`, we observed cases where empty `exec` commands could lead to ineffective retry/loop behavior.
- We implement two layers of protection:
  1) pre-call validation to reject empty command invocations,
  2) runtime circuit breaker to stop repeated tool failures and surface a deterministic failure signal.
- Additionally, UI tests were flaky on systems whose default locale is not English (weekday formatting output differed).

**Implementation Details**
- **Layer 1 (pre-tool-call guard)**  
  - Normalize tool name (`exec`/`bash`) and validate params.
  - If `command` is missing/empty/whitespace-only, return `{ blocked: true, reason }` and log a warning, preventing the call from running.
- **Layer 2 (consecutive-error circuit breaker)**  
  - Introduce state tracking for consecutive tool errors and an `onConsecutiveToolError` callback.
  - Increment on tool failures and trip after 3 consecutive errors; invoke the callback to stop/redirect the flow.
- **UI test stability**  
  - `formatNextRun()` now formats the weekday with a fixed locale (`en-US`) to make output deterministic across environments.

**Key Files Changed**
- `src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts` (block empty exec/bash command)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts` (error-state types)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.types.ts` (callback type)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts` (state initialization)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` (counter + threshold logic)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts` (runner integration)
- `ui/src/ui/presenter.ts` (locale-stable weekday formatting)

**Verification**
- `pnpm check` ✅
- `pnpm test` ✅
- `pnpm build` ✅
- Branch rebased onto latest `upstream/main` to keep the PR diff minimal and clean.

**Backward Compatibility**
- No behavior change for valid `exec`/`bash` calls.
- Invalid calls with empty/missing `command` are now rejected early with an explicit reason (intentional).

**Risk / Rollback**
- Risk: any external workflow relying on empty `exec` calls (considered invalid input) will now be blocked.
- Rollback: revert the pre-call guard and circuit-breaker additions; UI locale stabilization can be reverted independently.

**Checklist**
- [x] Focused PR scope (exec safety + error circuit breaker; UI is test-stability)
- [x] Up-to-date with upstream main
- [x] check/test/build passing
- [x] No secrets added

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, trim entries, drop blank/whitespace-only entries, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Normalize Gateway `agent.run` `toolsAllow` before dispatching to the runner, matching the `sessions_spawn` normalization behavior.
- Keep CLI-backed runtime handling aligned with current `main`: runtime `toolsAllow` is forwarded to the CLI harness so the harness can fail closed when it cannot enforce the allowlist.

## Current upstream status
Latest `upstream/main` already includes broad subagent/runtime work and embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support. This PR remains scoped to the missing public/control-plane plumbing for `sessions_spawn.toolsAllow`, plus Gateway normalization for raw `agent.run` inputs.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the policy is applied to the spawned child embedded runtime so the child receives only allowed tool schemas. Gateway `agent.run` also normalizes direct `toolsAllow` input before runner dispatch.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `8ffac4d1fc59032d23f7a97a2bf1a03752fbb3e6`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, local Gateway on loopback token auth, `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json` through Gateway (not `--local`), and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server that records every `/v1/responses` request tool schema.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local mock OpenAI Responses server on `127.0.0.1:19245`, started OpenClaw Gateway on `127.0.0.1:19246`, then ran the parent agent through Gateway:

```text
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-78441 OPENAI_API_KEY=mock node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json   --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent   --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].'   --timeout 90
```

The mock model returned a real `sessions_spawn` function call from the parent request with `toolsAllow:["read"]`; the Gateway run accepted it, spawned the child, and the child model request was logged from the same mock provider.

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head Gateway proof summarizer. The parent request had `sessions_spawn`; the spawned child request had only `read`, with `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, and `sessions_yield` absent.

```json
{
  "head": "8ffac4d1fc59032d23f7a97a2bf1a03752fbb3e6",
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
  "gatewayAgentRunId": "ba926aed-4c88-41bf-b00e-166c45c58f5a",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "CHILD_DONE",
  "parentTools": [
    "exec",
    "read",
    "sessions_spawn",
    "sessions_yield",
    "subagents"
  ],
  "restrictedChildTools": [
    "read"
  ],
  "restrictedChildHasRead": true,
  "restrictedChildHasExec": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsSpawn": false,
  "restrictedChildHasSessionsYield": false,
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.kEqyfu"
}
```

Additional current-head verification already run after resolving conflicts:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --testNamePattern toolsAllow --reporter=dot
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  8 passed | 300 skipped (308)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --testNamePattern 'toolsAllow|timestamp context|canonical Anthropic' --reporter=dot
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  30 passed | 488 skipped (518)

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Observed result after fix: A real Gateway-backed parent agent run called `sessions_spawn` with `toolsAllow:["read"]`, the child run completed with `CHILD_DONE`, and the child model request only advertised `read`. Disallowed parent/session/runtime tools were not present in the child request.

What was not tested: A paid external provider was not used. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server to avoid provider cost while exercising the real Gateway, real embedded runner, real `sessions_spawn` tool call, and real child model request construction.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`.
- Scenario locked in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported ACP runtime, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, is inherited by persisted child sessions, and is forwarded to CLI harnesses for fail-closed handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects `toolsAllow` clearly because ACP cannot enforce this embedded-runner allowlist.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. Unsupported ACP paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced. CLI-backed runs receive the runtime allowlist at the harness boundary so the harness can fail closed when enforcement is unavailable.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect all CLI harnesses to enforce runtime allowlists.
  - Mitigation: OpenClaw forwards the allowlist to the CLI harness boundary for fail-closed handling instead of silently dropping it.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: Agents: add compaction modes (warn, error, none) with proactive conte…
target.body:
Summary

   - Problem: Users often lose important conversation history due to aggressive
     auto-compaction, or they encounter cryptic provider-specific "context
     window exceeded" errors when the window is full.
   - Why it matters: Advanced users need explicit control over context
     management to prevent silent history loss and to receive clear, actionable
     warnings before a session fails.
   - What changed: Added three new compaction modes (warn, error, none) backed
     by a proactive context guard that estimates tokens before each LLM call.
     Integrated Greptile feedback to ensure the guard is robust even if token
     estimation fails.
   - What did NOT change (scope boundary): The existing logic for default and
     safeguard modes remains untouched.

  Change Type (select all)

   - [ ] Bug fix
   - [x] Feature
   - [ ] Refactor required for the fix
   - [x] Docs
   - [ ] Security hardening
   - [ ] Chore/infra

  Scope (select all touched areas)

   - [x] Gateway / orchestration
   - [ ] Skills / tool execution
   - [ ] Auth / tokens
   - [ ] Memory / storage
   - [ ] Integrations
   - [ ] API / contracts
   - [ ] UI / DX
   - [ ] CI/CD / infra

  Linked Issue/PR

   - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

   - Root cause: N/A (New feature)

  Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

   - Coverage level that should have caught this:
     - [x] Unit test (Verified via pnpm check and oxlint)
   - Target test or file: src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts
   - Scenario the test should lock in: Ensuring warn mode returns the cleanup
     message and error mode throws before hitting the LLM.
   - If no new test is added, why not: Manual verification was performed in a
     live environment to ensure the user-visible messages appear correctly in
     the messaging channel.

  User-visible / Behavior Changes

   - New configuration options in agents.defaults.compaction.mode: "warn",
     "error", and "none".
   - Users in warn mode will see a message: 🧹 Context near limit, use /compact
     instead of the agent failing or compacting automatically.

  Security Impact (required)

   - New permissions/capabilities? No
   - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
   - New/changed network calls? No
   - Command/tool execution surface changed? No
   - Data access scope changed? No

  Repro + Verification

  Environment

   - OS: macOS (darwin)
   - Runtime/container: Node.js 22.22.1
   - Model/provider: Verified with OpenAI/Anthropic providers.

  Steps

   1. Set agents.defaults.compaction.mode to "warn".
   2. Set a high reserveTokens (e.g., 100,000) to trigger the guard immediately.
   3. Send a message to the agent.

  Expected

   - The agent stops and returns: 🧹 Context near limit, use /compact.

  Actual

   - The agent correctly identifies the "near limit" state and returns the
     warning without calling the LLM.

  Evidence

   - [x] Trace/log snippets: Verified [compaction-guard] context near limit
     (mode=warn) logs in gateway.log.

  Human Verification (required)

   - Verified scenarios: warn mode (message returned), error mode (error
     thrown), and none mode (auto-compaction disabled).
   - Edge cases checked: Integrated Greptile feedback to handle estimateTokens
     failures; error mode now re-throws with cause, and warn mode provides a
     fallback warning instead of failing open.
   - What you did not verify: Latency impact on extremely large (1M+ tokens)
     histories, though estimateTokens is generally fast.

  Review Conversations

   - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in
     this PR.
   - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or
     maintainer judgment.

  Compatibility / Migration

   - Backward compatible? Yes (Defaults to default mode).
   - Config/env changes? Yes (New optional config fields).
   - Migration needed? No.

  Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

   - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Set compaction.mode back to
     "default" or "safeguard".
   - Files/config to restore: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.

  Risks and Mitigations

   - Risk: Token estimation failure could block the agent.
     - Mitigation: The guard is wrapped in a try/catch block. In warn mode, it
       falls back to a safe warning message; in error mode, it provides a
       descriptive error to the user.

target.title: Agents: add machine-readable session status time
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `session_status` only exposed human-readable local time, so reminder scheduling could not reliably compute exact timestamps.
- Why it matters: cron/reminder flows need machine-readable current time to convert requests like "in 30 minutes" into exact ISO-8601 schedules.
- What changed: added a local ISO timestamp formatter, appended local ISO + UTC time to the `session_status` time line, and added a regression test.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no system prompt injection changes, no cron scheduler behavior changes, and no auth/security surface changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix

- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #10841
- Related #7650
- Related #9899
- Related #10071

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`session_status` now includes machine-readable current time alongside the readable local time, for example local ISO plus UTC.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4
- Runtime/container: local zsh shell
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): agent `session_status`
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.userTimezone=America/Los_Angeles`, `agents.defaults.timeFormat=12`

### Steps

1. Call `session_status` for a session with a configured user timezone.
2. Inspect the `🕒 Time:` line in the returned status card.
3. Confirm it includes readable local time plus local ISO time and UTC time.

### Expected

- `session_status` returns exact machine-readable current time that an agent can use for reminder scheduling.

### Actual

- Before: only human-readable local time.
- After: human-readable local time plus `local ISO ...` and `UTC ...`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: checked the formatter output for `2026-02-08T03:15:00Z` and confirmed it renders `Saturday, February 7th, 2026 — 7:15 PM`, `2026-02-07T19:15:00-08:00`, and `2026-02-08T03:15:00Z`.
- Edge cases checked: local offset formatting for a non-UTC timezone; UTC seconds formatting.
- What you did **not** verify: in this Codex terminal environment, `pnpm test -- src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` and raw `vitest run` did not exit cleanly, so I did not get a clean local Vitest completion signal even though the regression test was added.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/date-time.ts`, `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: malformed time line formatting or missing timezone offset in `session_status`

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `Intl.DateTimeFormat(..., { timeZoneName: "longOffset" })` could behave differently on some runtimes.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): parse rich object file shares from webhook payload
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29152

When a user shares a file or image in Nextcloud Talk, the bot webhook receives the message but the NC Talk plugin was not parsing the rich object parameters — so the agent never saw the attachment.

NC Talk encodes file shares as rich object messages where `object.content` is a JSON string:
```json
{"message":"{file}","parameters":{"file":{"type":"file","id":"12345","name":"IMG_123.jpg","path":"Talk/IMG_123.jpg","link":"https://cloud.example.com/f/12345","mimetype":"image/jpeg","preview-available":"yes"}}}
```

Previously `payloadToInboundMessage()` passed this raw JSON string as `text`, leaving `mediaUrls` unpopulated.

## Changes

### `monitor.ts`
- Adds `parseRichObjectContent()` helper that detects JSON-encoded rich object payloads
- Extracts file links into `mediaUrls` (https/http only — rejects `file://`, `javascript:`, etc.)
- Resolves `{placeholder}` names for human-readable `displayText` (covers file shares, mentions, deck cards, polls, etc.)
- Uses `replaceAll` for placeholder substitution to handle duplicate occurrences
- Falls back to raw `content` for non-rich-object text — no behaviour change for regular messages

### `types.ts`
- Adds `mediaUrls?: string[]` to `NextcloudTalkInboundMessage`

### `inbound.ts`
- Passes `MediaUrls` into `finalizeInboundContext` (verified: already consumed natively as `string[]`)
- Relaxes the empty-body guard to allow media-only messages through (previously silently dropped)

## Tests

- `monitor.attachment.test.ts` — 7 unit tests: file share, text passthrough, malformed JSON fallback, name fallback, mention resolution, `file://` URL rejection, multi-file
- `monitor.live-integration.test.ts` — 5 integration tests using real HMAC-signed payloads against the webhook server

All 12 tests pass. TypeScript compiles clean (`tsc --noEmit`).

## Before / After

**Before:** Sharing a file in NC Talk → agent receives raw JSON string, never sees the attachment.

**After:** Sharing a file → agent receives `text = "filename.jpg"` and `mediaUrls = ["https://..."]` — can immediately process the image via vision without any manual workaround.
